primary_target: Bombing of bridge at Nyaungyan

The front page contains an extended air-combat narrative describing multiple Japanese fighter passes and the loss of plane #63.
special_remarks: 72 x 500 composition-B bombs were carried and all 72 were jettisoned due to enemy fighter attack. Ammunition expended was 15,200 rounds .50 cal, with an additional 5,000 rounds lost on crashed plane #63. Lt. primary_target: Railroad bridge at Chaung-U, Burma (21 57N, 95 17E)
route_summary: The front page identifies 81st Squadron with leading aircraft 21. Weather was good, with 6/10 cumulus, 2000-foot base, and 8-10 mile visibility. The first box bombed the Chaung-U bridge in multiple runs, with bombs released at 1001 and 1010 on headings of 190 and 200 degrees from about 1700 feet indicated at 190 IAS. The second box also made repeated runs, including a 0958 drop and a 1005 run on 200 to 250 degrees from about 2700 feet indicated at 185 IAS.

Results say the bridge was still standing on arrival; bombing likely weakened the northern support, damaged tracks at the northern end, and left the southern end appearing out of line. The formation also saw light and heavy AA about 5 miles north of Monywa, apparently directed at another squadron bombing there. Weather was 8/10 cumulus with 6000-foot base and 5-mile visibility; general weather was noted as fair for the primary and excellent for the alternate. First-box pattern was dropped in the Kennedy Peak target area at RU 213610; second box bombed Kalewa and its pattern ran roughly parallel to the river.
observation_points_raw: Kennedy Peak RU 209813; first-box pattern at RU 213610; Kalewa RU 828685; 23 15N 94 05E
bomb_load_carried: 120 x 250-lb GP with 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail fusing plus 16 x 250-lb delayed-action bombs
results_summary: were listed as unobserved for both primary and alternate. Photos were taken from aircraft 21, 20, and 23 with fixed K-24 cameras and aircraft 10 with a flexible K-20. Observations note Kalemyo appears destroyed and a fire was seen in the river at the fork, apparently not a ship and unidentified
photo_aircraft: were taken from aircraft 21, 20, and 23 with fixed K-24 cameras and aircraft 10 with a flexible K-20
special_remarks: Bomb load carried: 120 x 250-lb GP with 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail fusing plus 16 x 250-lb delayed-action bombs. Bombs dropped on target: 124 x 250-lb; aircraft #16 salvoed bombs at 23 15N, 94 05E because of bombing malfunction. First box bombed the primary at 1508 on true course 30 degrees from 12,300 feet at 175 IAS; due to cloud, bomb hits were impossible to observe. Second box bombed the alternate at Kalewa at 1532; about 98 percent of the pattern fell in the target area, beginning on the northwest side of the river and continuing parallel to it. Ammunition expended: 340 rounds .50 cal. Results were listed as unobserved for both primary and alternate. Photos were taken from aircraft 21, 20, and 23 with fixed K-24 cameras and aircraft 10 with a flexible K-20. Observations note Kalemyo appears destroyed and a fire was seen in the river at the fork, apparently not a ship and unidentified. Rule-linked candidate IMG_3553.JPG has not been treated as a confirmed back because it is itself a separate sortie front.

primary_target: Vital Corner, RU 205844, elevation 7750 feet
route_summary: Front-page route narrative is nil, but the sortie sheet records a Vital Corner strike from Feni under excellent weather with 3/10 stratocumulus, 6000-foot bases, and unlimited visibility. Two aircraft turned back early because of engine trouble: aircraft 19 at 23 08N, 92 25E at 0856 and aircraft 22 at 23 05N, 92 06E at 0852, with aircraft 22 down at 0905.
bomb_load_carried: 144 x 250-lb GP with 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail fuzing
photo_aircraft: with K-24 fixed cameras in aircraft 20 and 23 and K-20 flexible cameras in aircraft 10 and 13
special_remarks: Task notes describe the target as static troop concentrations. Bombs carried on takeoff: 144 x 250-lb GP with 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail fuzing. Bombs dropped on target: 120 x 250-lb; 24 were brought back by the two aircraft that turned home early. The first box bombed at 0940 from true altitude 11,750 feet at IAS 180 on a course of 76 degrees; the pattern covered the corner of the target and carried northeast down the side of the hill. The second box bombed at 0941 from true altitude 12,300 feet at IAS 180 on a course of 76 degrees; the pattern started northeast of the target on the side of the hill and carried into the valley. Ammunition expended: 440 rounds .50 cal. Damage was not observed due to cloud formation and the heaviest concentration of the pattern was beyond the target, so little damage was thought done. Light small-arms fire was seen over the target area; casualties nil. Photographs were taken with K-24 fixed cameras in aircraft 20 and 23 and K-20 flexible cameras in aircraft 10 and 13. Observations include a dense black fire northwest of the target at 23 20N, 93 45E at 0922, possibly from a burning aircraft or gasoline, and friendly aircraft in the area including Hurribombers, C-47s, Spitfires, and P-47s. Source image IMG_3524.JPG was reviewed directly and is a separate 81st Squadron front page, not the reverse of IMG_3523.JPG.

casualties: were recorded on the visible front

primary_target: Natmauk railroad bridge no. 162 at PU 7875, elevation 600 feet
alternate_target: any railroad bridge on the Pyinmana-Kyaukpadaung rail line
route_summary: Escort line records 12 P-38s. The sortie attacked Natmauk bridge under good weather with 2/10 stratocumulus, 5000-foot base, and 10-12 mile visibility. Aircraft 29 turned back due to engine trouble at 1018 at 21 31N, 93 16E, jettisoned its bombs safe at 1026 at 21 45N, 92 58E, and landed back at Feni at 1130.
bomb_load_carried: 40 x 1000-lb Comp
results_summary: state the center span appeared to be sagging slightly though the bridge was still standing, and some damage was thought accomplished. Casualties nil. Photographs of the target were taken from fixed K-24 cameras in aircraft 23, 27, and 20 and from flexible K-20 cameras in aircraft 12 and 25
leaflet_drops: after bombs were away included 12,000 SB/15, 10,000 SB/17, 2500 SBN/4, and 10,000 pilots-warning leaflets
special_remarks: The bridge was reported undamaged and serviceable upon arrival. Bombs carried on takeoff: 40 x 1000-lb Comp. B with 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail fuzing. Bombs dropped on target: 37 x 1000-lb; due to a malfunction, the bombs in aircraft 27 did not release at the proper time and were salvoed in the target area, with the pattern striking the village on the north side of the river. The first box bombed at 1127 from true altitude 4400 feet on a course of 356 degrees at IAS 185; the pattern started short of the bridge on the western side and carried into the river about the center of the bridge, with two bombs apparently striking the left-center support. The second box bombed at 1129 from true altitude 4000 feet on a heading of 0 degrees at IAS 185; the bombs were likewise short and carried to the west-center portion of the bridge, with one strike near enough to the center span to have caused damage. Ammunition expended: 465 rounds .50 cal. Results state the center span appeared to be sagging slightly though the bridge was still standing, and some damage was thought accomplished. Casualties nil. Photographs of the target were taken from fixed K-24 cameras in aircraft 23, 27, and 20 and from flexible K-20 cameras in aircraft 12 and 25. Leaflets dropped after bombs were away included 12,000 SB/15, 10,000 SB/17, 2500 SBN/4, and 10,000 pilots-warning leaflets. About 40 M/T were seen on the road at PU 778625, 20 to 30 rail cars were seen at Kyaukpadaung railhead PP 450291, and rolling stock was reported all along the rail line from Taungdwingyi at PU 908285 north to Kyaukpadaung. A radio operator also heard what he thought were B-24s under fighter attack and one aircraft requesting information on whether it could land on an island, but the location was unknown. Rule-linked candidate IMG_3547.JPG was reviewed directly and is a separate 83rd Bomb Squadron front rather than the reverse of IMG_3546.JPG.

primary_target: Bomb railroad bridge north of Taungdwingyi, Burma at PU 8833
route_summary: Mission from Fenny against the railroad bridge north of Taungdwingyi under good weather with 2/10 to 4/10 cumulus, 3500-foot base, and good visibility. Route weather on the return matched outbound conditions, with 3/10 cumulus to the Chin Hills and 3/10 to 4/10 cumulus over the hills.
bomb_load_carried: 44 x 1000-lb Comp
results_summary: state the first-box pattern, bombing on leader, struck 300 feet short of the bridge
opposition_summary: nil. A/C 44 crashed at 0905 with complete loss of crew and plane, noted as about two miles southwest of Fenny town
photo_aircraft: aircraft included 30 with K-24 (14 shots), 31 with K-3B (3 shots), 49 with K-17 (7 shots), and 39 with K-24 (10 shots) plus K-20 (5 shots)
special_remarks: Bombs carried: 44 x 1000-lb Comp. B with 1/10 nose fuse and 1/40 tail fuse. Bombs on target: 40. Bombs lost in crash of aircraft 44: 4. Ammunition expended: 504 rounds .50 calibre, test fired. First-box bombing was on course 52 degrees at 175 IAS from 5000 feet with 30-foot interval; second-box course 90 degrees at 190 IAS from 5400 feet with 30-foot interval. Results state the first-box pattern, bombing on leader, struck 300 feet short of the bridge; the second-box pattern, also on leader, struck about 150 feet east of the bridge. Opposition nil. A/C 44 crashed at 0905 with complete loss of crew and plane, noted as about two miles southwest of Fenny town. Photo aircraft included 30 with K-24 (14 shots), 31 with K-3B (3 shots), 49 with K-17 (7 shots), and 39 with K-24 (10 shots) plus K-20 (5 shots). Observations note eight rail cars at 20 16N, 95 26E (PU 7960) at 1125 and 20-30 small boats at 1053 at 20 40N, 94 46E (PP 0410). Rule-linked candidate IMG_3550.JPG has not yet been reviewed.

primary_target: Bombing bridges on Ye-U - Kin-U road at SR 2420, SR 1819, and SR 1420
route_summary: Route was direct from Fenny to target and direct return to base. Weather from base to hills was 4/10 to 6/10 cumulus with 2500-foot base and 8000-foot tops; over hills 4/10 to 6/10 cumulus to 16,000; in the valley 1/10 to 3/10 scattered cumulus with 3000-foot base and 4000-foot tops; target area clear.
bomb_load_carried: 144 x 250-lb GP fused 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail
results_summary: state the first element bombed the bridge at SR 2420 and its pattern extended across the bridge, destroying the east end. The second element attacked the bridge at SR 1819, with its pattern extending over and south of the bridge. The third element did not sight the bridge at SR 2420 and instead attacked the bridge at SR 2221, its pattern falling over and west of the bridge
opposition_summary: and casualties were nil. Photo aircraft for the first box were 58 and 57
photo_aircraft: aircraft for the first box were 58 and 57
special_remarks: Bomb load carried: 144 x 250-lb GP fused 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail. Bombs on target: 144 x 250-lb GP. Bombing interval 20 feet. Bombing courses were listed by element as 70 degrees, 240 degrees, 35 degrees, and 230 degrees. Speed 190 MPH; altitude 4500 feet. Ammunition expended: 720 rounds .50 cal. Results state the first element bombed the bridge at SR 2420 and its pattern extended across the bridge, destroying the east end. The second element attacked the bridge at SR 1819, with its pattern extending over and south of the bridge. The third element did not sight the bridge at SR 2420 and instead attacked the bridge at SR 2221, its pattern falling over and west of the bridge. The fourth element attacked the bridge at SR 2420, its bombs falling over and east of the target. Leaflets dropped at SR 1924 included 10,000 CBA/31, 7,500 SBN/4, 5,000 SJN/9, 40,000 SB/17, and 9,000 SB/15. Opposition and casualties were nil. Photo aircraft for the first box were 58 and 57; for the second box 54, 56, and 64. Observations note the Ye-U road bridge was half in the water, there was no canal or bridge visible at SR 1420, built-up dikes and gates were seen at SR 2716 apparently controlling Shwebo Canal irrigation, a metal-roofed building probable to be a factory stood at the west edge of Kin-U, 12 rail cars were noticed at Kin-U, and one small boat was observed at 23 04N, 94 05E. Rule-linked candidate IMG_3548.JPG has not yet been reviewed.

Plane 75 developed engine trouble at 1135 at 22 57N, 95 53E and landed at Tamu, Burma at 1300 with its starboard engine unserviceable; the crew was reported OK and was being taken to Imphal by light plane that afternoon. Target weather was listed as cumulus 3/10 to 5/10 with 5000-foot base, 10-mile visibility, and excellent general weather.
bomb_load_carried: 80 x 500-lb M-64 GP with 1/10-second nose and 1/40-second tail fuzing
results_summary: state the first-box pattern straddled the bridge on either side, probably knocking one span out of line. The second-box pattern landed at either end of the bridge causing no damage, with six bombs falling 300 yards southwest of the bridge. Opposition consisted of 6-10 bursts of accurate heavy AA on the bomb run, with flashes seen from one gun located just west of three small shacks about 25 yards west of the stream

primary_target: Road bridge at Hsipaw, Burma (22 37N, 97 17E), ST 2115
route_summary: Mission from Fenny against the Hsipaw road bridge under good target weather: 3/10 cumulus, base 3000 feet, tops 4000 feet, visibility good, general weather good. Map references include Arakan and Mandalay 1:1,000,000, NF 47/1 1:500,000, and 93 F quarter-inch sheets.
bomb_load_carried: 74 x 500-lb GP with 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail fusing
bombs_returned: 6 after aircraft 38 went back early because of mechanical failure
bombing_altitude: 3000 feet | 4000 feet | 9000 feet indicated | 9200 feet indicated
results_summary: state the second-box pattern struck just left of the southeast approach to the bridge and carried through one-half to two-thirds of the bridge, with at least one hit on the first span. The first-box pattern fell in a field northwest of the bridge but still scored about 20 hits on road or bridge. Small-arms fire was reported from the Shwebo area at SR 3601
photo_aircraft: aircraft included 30 with K-24 (6 shots), 48 with K-17 (5 shots), and 49 with K-24 (14 shots)
leaflet_drops: 1 bundle SJN/9 (2500 copies), 3 bundles SJ/43 (30,000 copies), and 1 bundle SB/17 (4000 copies)
special_remarks: Bomb load carried: 74 x 500-lb GP with 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail fusing. Bombs dropped: 68. Bombs returned: 6 after aircraft 38 went back early because of mechanical failure. Ammunition expended: 450 rounds .50 calibre, test fired. First-box bombing course was 6 degrees at 177 IAS from 9000 feet indicated; second-box course 5 degrees at 180 IAS from 9200 feet indicated, with interval settings of 40 feet for lead aircraft and 20 feet for wing aircraft. Leaflets dropped: 1 bundle SJN/9 (2500 copies), 3 bundles SJ/43 (30,000 copies), and 1 bundle SB/17 (4000 copies). Results state the second-box pattern struck just left of the southeast approach to the bridge and carried through one-half to two-thirds of the bridge, with at least one hit on the first span. The first-box pattern fell in a field northwest of the bridge but still scored about 20 hits on road or bridge. Small-arms fire was reported from the Shwebo area at SR 3601; casualties nil. Observations note a 150-foot boat with superstructure docked at SR 6904 at 1017; a boat or barge about one-quarter the length of the Hsipaw bridge docked parallel to its southeast side; and an airfield at SW 4705 northeast of Shwebo in good condition, with five or more unidentified silver aircraft parked among trees off the field. Rule-linked candidate IMG_3536.JPG has not yet been reviewed.

primary_target: Bomb railroad bridge #433, 7 miles northeast of Hsipaw, Burma
route_summary: Route note states direct to target and direct return to base. Weather from base to hills was 2/10 to 3/10 cumulus with 3000-foot bases and 5000-foot tops; over hills 6/10 cumulus from hilltops to 8000 feet; in the valley 4/10 cumulus with bases 2500 and tops 9000; over the hills again 6/10 cumulus up to 12,000; target area 2/10 cumulus with 3000-foot bases and 12,000-foot tops, with Chin Hills built up to 12,000 on return.
bomb_load_carried: 84 x 500-lb GP fused 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail
bombs_salvoed_or_jettisoned: 8 x 500-lb GP, with plane 64 salvoing its bombs 400 feet southwest of the target because of electrical or mechanical failure
results_summary: state the first-box pattern fell over and west of the bridge and the second-box pattern straddled the south end of the bridge. 70,000 SJ/43 leaflets were dropped at 22 52N, 95 38E. Opposition consisted of three heavy anti-aircraft guns accurate for altitude and course, firing about 15 bursts
opposition_summary: consisted of three heavy anti-aircraft guns accurate for altitude and course, firing about 15 bursts
photo_aircraft: aircraft for the first box were 57 and 54
special_remarks: Bomb load carried: 84 x 500-lb GP fused 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail. Bombs on target: 68 x 500-lb GP. Bombs jettisoned: 8 x 500-lb GP, with plane 64 salvoing its bombs 400 feet southwest of the target because of electrical or mechanical failure. Bombs brought back: 8 x 500-lb GP. Bombing interval was 40 feet for lead ships and 20 feet for wing planes; bombing course 350 degrees; speed 190 MPH; altitude 9000 feet. Ammunition expended: 650 rounds .50 cal. Results state the first-box pattern fell over and west of the bridge and the second-box pattern straddled the south end of the bridge. 70,000 SJ/43 leaflets were dropped at 22 52N, 95 38E. Opposition consisted of three heavy anti-aircraft guns accurate for altitude and course, firing about 15 bursts; two guns were located at the north end of the bridge and one at the south end, with additional ground fire from the bridge area and from ST 2915. Plane 51 received eight holes in its left wing and one in the left side of the fuselage, and plane 56 received one hole in its left nacelle and two holes in the nose. Casualties were nil. Photo aircraft for the first box were 57 and 54; for the second box 61, 55, and 64. Observations include one 75-foot boat and two small boats stationary by the east bank of the Chindwin River at 22 56N, 94 28E at 1145. Remarks state plane 60 turned back to base at 23 05N, 92 20E due to a bad right engine. Rule-linked candidate IMG_3538.JPG has not yet been reviewed.

primary_target: Maymyo railway yards and sheds, Burma
alternate_target: rail yards and troops billets (#3 area), Monywa, Burma
route_summary: Mission launched in two six-plane boxes. Aircraft 83, 85, and 88 returned about 10 minutes after takeoff because of mechanical trouble in lead aircraft 83, landing 0846 with all bombs. Remaining aircraft flew toward the primary on heading 101 degrees, diverted to the alternate when Maymyo weather was bad on a heading of 268 degrees, and returned to base on heading 290 degrees. Bomb run at Monywa was on 160 degrees from 8300 feet indicated at 185 IAS with 225-foot interval setting, pattern bombing on formation leader.
weather_summary: on the front reads cumulus 8-9/10 with 6000-foot base, tops 11000, 10-mile visibility, general weather very good. Bomb load carried on takeoff: 82 x 500-lb M-64 GP fused 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail. Of the 62 bombs that reached the target area after early returns, 56 were dropped in the target area, 6 were salvoed unarmed 150 yards south of target but were seen to explode, and 20 bombs were brought back to base. Ammunition expended: 572 rounds .50 cal estimated test fire
bomb_load_carried: 82 x 500-lb M-64 GP fused 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail
results_summary: state all but six of the 62 bombs carried to the target fell in the assigned area just west of, and not including, the railroad yards
opposition_summary: moderate inaccurate light AA from Monywa town with white bursts and moderate inaccurate heavy AA
photo_aircraft: were taken on bomb-run statistics by aircraft 72, 77, 84, and 86
leaflet_drops: 72, 77, 84, and 86. 20,000 SB/17 propaganda leaflets were dropped in the target area
special_remarks: Target weather on the front reads cumulus 8-9/10 with 6000-foot base, tops 11000, 10-mile visibility, general weather very good. Bomb load carried on takeoff: 82 x 500-lb M-64 GP fused 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail. Of the 62 bombs that reached the target area after early returns, 56 were dropped in the target area, 6 were salvoed unarmed 150 yards south of target but were seen to explode, and 20 bombs were brought back to base. Ammunition expended: 572 rounds .50 cal estimated test fire. Results state all but six of the 62 bombs carried to the target fell in the assigned area just west of, and not including, the railroad yards; direct hits were observed on many buildings and a large fire with heavy black smoke followed the flames. Opposition: moderate inaccurate light AA from Monywa town with white bursts and moderate inaccurate heavy AA; meager inaccurate heavy AA from near Alon. Casualties nil. Photos were taken on bomb-run statistics by aircraft 72, 77, 84, and 86. 20,000 SB/17 propaganda leaflets were dropped in the target area. Heavy billowing black smoke was seen rising to 6000 feet from the town as formation left the area, likely from another squadron's attack.

primary_target: Maymyo military area, LG 0172, elevation 3357 feet
route_summary: Primary Maymyo target was not attacked and the alternate at Monywa was bombed instead. First box bombed at 1112 from true altitude about 8500 feet on course 180 degrees at IAS 197 to 200; pattern started at the edge of the target and carried directly across with about 90% of bombs in the target area. Second box bombed at 1114; its pattern started just to the right of the first-box pattern and carried directly across the target, with a few bombs falling into the river. Course, altitude, and speed were stated to be the same for the second box.
map_reference: quarter-inch sheets 93 B and 84 N plus aerial mosaic of Monywa and Maymyo
weather_summary: strato-cumulus 2/10, cloud base 6000 feet, visibility unlimited, general weather excellent. Map references: quarter-inch sheets 93 B and 84 N plus aerial mosaic of Monywa and Maymyo. Front states target was undamaged on arrival. Bombs carried on takeoff: 74 x 500-lb GP with 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail fusing
bomb_load_carried: 74 x 500-lb GP with 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail fusing
results_summary: note no fires or smoke visible on leaving target, but one bomb was seen to hit next to a large building and it was felt this building was damaged. Opposition: flashes from six anti-aircraft batteries
opposition_summary: flashes from six anti-aircraft batteries
photo_aircraft: with K-24 cameras from aircraft 20, 23, and 27 and from a K-20 camera in aircraft 12
special_remarks: OpRep 139. Leading aircraft was #21. Weather: strato-cumulus 2/10, cloud base 6000 feet, visibility unlimited, general weather excellent. Map references: quarter-inch sheets 93 B and 84 N plus aerial mosaic of Monywa and Maymyo. Front states target was undamaged on arrival. Bombs carried on takeoff: 74 x 500-lb GP with 1/10 nose and 1/40 tail fusing; bombs dropped on target: 54 x 500. Due to malfunctions, several aircraft salvoed bombs: aircraft 10 salvoed safe at 22 30N, 93 25E, turned back at 0957 at 22 32N, 94 10E, and landed at 1050; aircraft 17 salvoed armed at 22 43N, 91 20E, turned back at 22 55N, 91 58E at 0920, and landed at 1000; aircraft 23 salvoed at 22 31N, 94 25E at 1134 and continued with the formation. The narrative attributes the malfunctions to engine trouble in aircraft 10, a faulty turret in aircraft 17, and faulty bomb release in aircraft 23. Leaflets dropped: SJ/43 20,000 and SJN/10 2,500. Ammunition expended: 365 rounds .50 calibre. Results note no fires or smoke visible on leaving target, but one bomb was seen to hit next to a large building and it was felt this building was damaged. Opposition: flashes from six anti-aircraft batteries; by the report's identification these appear to match Japanese AA defence numbers 4 and 5 at the target and numbers 1 and 2 on the southwest side of the river. Heavy AA was seen to the left of target; otherwise flak was light, moderate, and inaccurate. Casualties nil. Photographs were obtained with K-24 cameras from aircraft 20, 23, and 27 and from a K-20 camera in aircraft 12.
